textPlain:
I don’t know if taking energy from the body is a good idea.
The laws of thermodynamics will require you to compensate. Also playing with very complex systems like a human body, neglecting what the side effects might be, is unwise on a good day.

We're approaching the point where someone will put together every form of energy harvest, solar, kinetic, temperature, air pressure (from wind, etc) and just store it in a super-capacitor for whatever you are wearing/holding, watch, phone etc.Garmin already has solar on many watches to extend battery and the Kinefox is already doing kinetic on animal trackinghttps://journals.plos.org/plosone/article/figure/image?downl...
